title: EARLY ASSESSMENT AND SUPPORT ALLIANCE - 211info
text: NEW DIRECTIONS NORTHWEST INC OUTPATIENT PROGRAM EARLY ASSESSMENT AND SUPPORT ALLIANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides rapid identification, support, assessment and treatment for teenagers and young adults who are experiencing the early signs of psychosis. Services include education about psychosis; medication along with medication monitoring; individual and group counseling; multi-family groups; occupational therapy; family education and support; vocational training and life-skills coaching; 24/7 crisis services; connections to community resources. 3425 13TH Street Baker City, OR 97814 Eligibility: Ages 12-30 Who have had a first episode of psychosis within the last 12 months or who are experiencing early at-risk symptoms for psychosis and their families Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 519-3239 . Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (541) 523-7400 Main phone (541) 519-7126 Crisis Line Main phone (541) 519-3239 Karyn Wallace [email protected] Reception (541) 523-7400 Emergency (541) 519-7126 Crisis Line Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call main or reception number for intake or visit website FEES: Accepts OHP and private insurances. Sliding scale fees available for uninsured patients. No one will be denied access to outpatient mental health or addictions services due to inability to pay. DOCUMENTS: Insurance information if applicable. Proof of income if applying for sliding scale fees. AREA SERVED: Baker County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Exit 304 off of I-84 Services: Early Intervention for Mental Illness Other Services Offered At This Location: COUNSELING SETTINGS - OUTPATIENT MENTAL HEALTH AND SUD ADDICTIONS EMPLOYMENT - SUPPORTED EMPLOYMENT MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - BAKER COUNTY CRISIS LINE REHABILITATION/HABILITATION SERVICES - COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES PROGRAM SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - ASSERTIVE COMMUNITY TREATMENT If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
